title: RDF Triple
description: Schema describing an RDF triple (statement) as the fundamental unit of data in the Resource Description Framework. A triple consists of a subject, predicate, and object that together express a relationship between resources.

subject.description: The resource being described, expressed as an IRI (Internationalized Resource Identifier) or blank node identifier.

predicate.description: The property or relationship connecting the subject to the object, expressed as an IRI.

oneOf.description: An IRI reference to another resource.

object.description: The value or resource that the subject is related to via the predicate. Can be an IRI, blank node, or literal value.

literal.description: An RDF literal value with optional datatype and language tag.

value.description: The lexical form of the literal.

datatype.description: The IRI identifying the datatype of the literal, defaulting to xsd:string.

language.description: A BCP47 language tag for language-tagged strings.
